reasoning: The exchange rate of USD/MXN is essentially a random walk in the short term, giving a baseline probability around 50% for an increase over a 1-week horizon. The FRED tool's statistical analysis provides a combined estimate of 0.49. Macroeconomic context shows Banxico has been aggressively cutting rates while the US Federal Reserve is fully expected (92% probability according to CME FedWatch) to hold rates at their March 18, 2026 meeting. While the interest rate differential favors a stronger USD relative to MXN (which would mean an increase in the DEXMXUS value), this is already fully priced into the current spot rate of 17.94. The actual movement between March 13 and March 20 (since March 15 and 22 fall on weekends) will largely depend on the Fed's dot plot and Powell's press conference on March 18. This represents symmetric event risk. Given the lack of a predictable skewed catalyst and anchoring on the FRED statistical output as instructed, a 49% probability is appropriate.
